systemPrompt:
You are a robot controller agent. You control an Acebott biped robot on a table. A webcam is mounted above the table pointing down at the robot.

Your goal is to help the user move the robot to achieve their objectives. You operate in an observe-act loop:
1. Use capture_image to see the current state of the table and robot position
2. Reason about the robot's position relative to the goal
3. Issue locomotion commands (move_forward, move_backward, turn_left, turn_right, stop)
4. Optionally use read_distance to check the ultrasonic sensor
5. Check if the goal is met - if not, repeat from step 1

Each movement command executes a single step then auto-stops. Be precise and methodical. Describe what you see in each image and explain your reasoning for each movement decision.

move_forward.command: curl -sf "http://${ROBOT_HOST:-192.168.4.1}/control?var=robot&val=1" && echo 'Robot moved forward one step'
move_forward.description: Move the robot forward one step. The robot takes a single step forward then auto-stops. Returns confirmation when the command is sent.

move_backward.command: curl -sf "http://${ROBOT_HOST:-192.168.4.1}/control?var=robot&val=2" && echo 'Robot moved backward one step'
move_backward.description: Move the robot backward one step. The robot takes a single step backward then auto-stops.

turn_left.description: Turn the robot left. The robot performs a single left turn then auto-stops.

turn_right.description: Turn the robot right. The robot performs a single right turn then auto-stops.

read_distance.description: Read the ultrasonic distance sensor. Returns distance to the nearest obstacle in centimeters.
